thinks.think: The video cuts from black to reveal a bald man with black-rimmed glasses facing the camera. He wears a purple suit jacket, a light blue checkered shirt, and a blue tie. The background is an out-of-focus indoor hallway with a door frame and a small green light visible on the wall to the left.

summary.text: [0-4] A bald man with black-rimmed glasses, wearing a purple suit jacket, light blue checkered shirt, and blue tie, faces the camera against a blurred hallway background with a green light. He speaks while maintaining eye contact. [4-12] The shot cuts to a close-up profile of the man facing right against a solid red wall. He continues speaking, his expression shifting from a slight smile to an open mouth showing teeth. [12-14] The view returns to the front-facing medium shot in the hallway. The man smiles as white text "15-YEA" appears on the lower right of his jacket.

[14-20] A medium shot captures a bald man with black-rimmed glasses in a purple suit and blue tie against a blurred hallway. Text overlays identify him as "JAMAL MAYER," with the subtitle changing from "15-YEAR NHL VETERAN" to "BLACKHAWKS COMMUNITY LIAISON."
[20-24] The scene cuts to a close-up profile of the man facing right against a red wall, speaking without text.
[24-28] A still photo shows a young boy against a white cinder block wall wearing a red and white jersey with "GEORGE BELL MINOR HOCKEY" text. He holds a hockey glove to his chest and smiles.

[28-30] A static photograph displays a young boy wearing a red and white hockey jersey with "GEORGE BELL MINOR HOCKEY" text, holding a glove against a white cinder block wall. [30-36] The scene cuts to a bald man with thick black-rimmed glasses wearing a purple suit jacket and checkered shirt. He is first shown in close-up profile against a red wall, then in a medium front-facing shot revealing a blue tie and a blurred hallway background. [36-42] The video transitions to a red graphic background featuring the Chicago Blackhawks logo and the text "ORIGINALS." Finally, sponsor branding appears with "AND," the Mazda logo, "MAZDA OF ORLAND PARK," and "PRESENT" overlaid on faint blue hockey rink diagrams.

[42-50] A red title screen displays "MAKING" in black text on the left and "STRIDES" in white text on the right, flanking a central Chicago Blackhawks logo against a background of faint blue hockey rink diagrams. The graphic remains static after an initial transition. [50-54] The visual content cuts abruptly to a solid black screen, removing all text and graphics for the remainder of the clip.

[56-58] The camera zooms out from the brick facade of Manuel Perez, Jr. Elementary School. An American flag waves left while snow covers the ground near a black metal fence right.
[58-62] A static shot shows a light blue mosaic mural of two children studying. Text reads "mis raíces son mi inspiración" at the top and "my roots are my inspiration" along the bottom.
[62-64] The camera focuses on a reddish-brown brick wall with white painted text: "todos vivimos aqui" above "we all live here," with snow at the base.
[64-66] A wooden door features a red apple sign reading "WELCOME" in yellow with Chinese characters. Vertical orange banners with Chinese text hang on the right glass pane.
[66-68] Inside, a hallway mural depicts a man with a mustache in a dark suit wearing a green, white, and red sash. To the left, a door displays a blue "NOTICE" sign.

[68-70] A static shot displays an interior brick wall mural of a man with a mustache wearing a dark suit and a green, white, and red sash. To the left, a wooden door features a blue "NOTICE" sign, and a grey building illustration is painted behind the figure. [70-78] A red graphic background appears with faint blue hockey rink diagrams. White text states, "The Get Out and Learn (G.O.A.L.) program has given more than 275,000 Chicago Public School kids access to street hockey equipment and instruction." The number "275,000" is displayed prominently in large, bold white font in the center. The graphic remains static.

[78-80] A static red graphic displays white text reading 'The Get Out and Learn (G.O.A.L.) program has given more than 275,000 Chicago Public School kids access to street hockey equipment and instruction' against faint blue hockey rink diagrams. [80-84] The text updates to 'Perez Elementary is one of more than 550 SCHOOLS visited through G.O.A.L.,' with '550 SCHOOLS' emphasized in large, bold, italicized font. [84-88] The scene cuts to a classroom where a white t-shirt featuring a 'FIRST STRIDE' logo and Blackhawks emblem hangs in the foreground, while children sit at desks near bright windows in the background. [88-90] The view shifts to students seated at desks from behind, wearing matching navy blue shirts, with one student sporting a long dark braid.

[90-94] A young girl with dark hair in a navy blue long-sleeved shirt sits at a desk holding a yellow pencil in each hand, writing in an open notebook beside a silver laptop and green pen container before lifting her head to look right. [94-96] An extreme close-up captures a hand gripping a yellow pencil, writing on white paper. [96-102] A close-up shows two children in navy blue shirts; the child on the left rests a hand against their neck with their mouth open, while the child on the right faces forward, transitioning from a neutral expression to having their mouth slightly open.

[102-106] Two students in navy blue shirts are shown in close-up; one keeps a hand on their neck with an open mouth while the other faces forward. [106-110] The angle changes to show a student in profile wearing a gold necklace seated next to another holding a yellow pencil; both look down at their work before the first raises the pencil to their temple. [110-112] A girl with dark hair tied back, wearing a navy shirt with white lettering, speaks in a medium shot with a whiteboard behind her. [112-116] The scene cuts to three students at desks; a girl on the right with a ponytail smiles holding a pencil, facing a student with their back to the camera who raises an arm, while a central student rests their chin on their hand.

[116-122] A close-up captures a girl with dark hair tied back, wearing a navy blue shirt with white lettering and a gold chain necklace. She smiles widely, looking downward while holding a yellow pencil, with a blurred figure in the background. [122-126] Hands are seen inserting a yellow pencil into a blue pencil sharpener with a silver metal faceplate containing four circular holes. The hand rotates the pencil within the device. [126-128] The scene shows a blue surface with the white text "MANUEL PEREZ JR." in capital serif font, with yellow fabric on the left and colorful strips on the right. [128-130] A woman with long blonde hair, wearing a teal blazer over a black top and a thin gold necklace, speaks directly to the camera. Behind her is a blue bulletin board decorated with a large paper tree with orange leaves.

[130-134] Inside a bus with grey seats and white ceiling, a child in a blue vest smiles while a boy in a blue jacket turns back. [134-136] In a room with white block walls, children in navy blue shirts stand in line, including a girl with dark curly hair and a pink clip near an adult's arm. [136-140] Two boys in navy tops sit side-by-side; the left wears a crewneck with white text, the right a polo. They look forward, open mouths as if singing, then close them. [140-142] A red display board features folders labeled "Alejandra" and "Cassie" holding papers titled "my family's traditions" with colorful drawings.

[142-146] A close-up reveals a mural on a black brick wall featuring a figure with a large yellow headdress with orange accents and a white outline. The figure has orange eyes, a brown arm wearing a yellow and blue wristband, and clothing with blue and white geometric triangle patterns.
[146-148] A girl in a white t-shirt sits with hands clasped near her chin, looking upward, beside a smiling boy in a navy blue shirt and a girl in a navy shirt with white lettering.
[148-152] A woman with long blonde hair, wearing a teal blazer over a black top and a gold necklace, speaks in front of a blue bulletin board featuring a paper tree with orange and green leaves.
[152-154] A mosaic artwork displays two children with dark hair in blue shirts writing on white paper at a table, set against a background of yellow and blue geometric tiles and a sun shape.

[156-158] A close-up captures a hand holding a pencil, writing on white lined notebook paper under dim lighting. [158-160] The view cuts to a girl with dark hair pulled back, wearing a navy blue shirt with white lettering, looking forward with a focused expression while another student is blurred in the foreground. [160-164] A static wide shot shows a large framed painting on a white wall depicting a colorful landscape with a bridge, river, and buildings. Visible text includes "HOMENAJE" and "EL HOMBRE PINTO AL RIO." Below, a sign reads "PEREZ SCHOOL" and "PORTFOLIO DESIGN PROJECT." [164-166] A bronze bust of a man with short hair and crossed arms stands before a dark blue curtain. He wears a collared shirt. To the left, an American flag and a white and blue flag are displayed, with green wreaths flanking the statue.

From timestamp 168 to 178, the video features a woman with long blonde hair wearing a teal blazer over a black top. She speaks directly to the camera continuously throughout the segment. In the lower right corner, white text reading "JESSICA JOHNSON PRINCIPAL" is displayed and remains visible. The background features a blue bulletin board with a colorful decorative border. On the board, there is a large paper tree decoration with orange leaves. The woman maintains her position and continues her address to the viewer. The scene remains static with no changes in the background or the woman's attire. The text overlay stays fixed in place. The focus remains on the woman's face and upper body as she talks. The observations confirm she continues speaking in each segment. The lighting is bright and even. She is framed from the chest up. Her long hair falls over her shoulders.

[166-168] Jessica Johnson, labeled "JESSICA JOHNSON PRINCIPAL," speaks in a teal blazer before a blue board with an orange tree. [178-180] Backpacks, including a pink bag with a heart-eyes emoji and others with blue/orange patterns, sit on a tiled floor. [180-182] A student in a navy sweatshirt with a white "P" writes on paper with a yellow pencil. [182-184] A man in a gym wears a navy shirt reading "I TEACH" above "BASKETBALL SOFTBALL FLAGFOOTBALL VOLLEYBALL BADMINTON MOTIVATE RUN SWIMMING HEALTH KICKBALL GOLF". Blue padding with a black panther logo is behind him.

[186-190] A close-up captures a person's hands writing in a spiral notebook with a black pen. The left hand, adorned with a silver ring on the ring finger, points at the paper while the right hand writes. To the right, a can of Dei Fratelli Tomato Sauce with a red and white label displaying "Dei Fratelli", "Tomato SAUCE", and "All Natural" sits on the desk surface. The background remains dark and indistinct. [190-194] The shot changes to a close-up of two young girls with dark hair. The girl on the left is in focus, wearing a dark top, with her mouth slightly open. The girl on the right is blurred in the foreground, facing left. They appear seated together in a dimly lit environment.

[184-186] A man with short brown hair and light stubble speaks in a gymnasium, wearing a navy blue t-shirt displaying "I TEACH" in large white letters above a list of colorful sports. Behind him, blue wall padding features a black panther logo near a volleyball net pole. [194-198] A woman with long blonde hair in a teal blazer over a black top speaks to the camera. Behind her is a blue bulletin board with a paper tree featuring orange leaves. Her mouth moves as she articulates words. [198-200] A pink paper titled "Student of the month" is pinned to a dark surface, displaying a photo of a smiling girl alongside the text "February, 2018" and the name "Dayanara Gutierrez" in script font. The initials "Ms. J." are written at the bottom right. Partially visible to the right is a decoration featuring red hearts and the letters "Fe".

[202-204] Hands interact with a spiral-bound notebook on a wooden desk as a hand lifts a sheet of white lined paper. A yellow pencil rests on the surface, and another person's hands and green sleeve are partially visible in the lower right corner. [204-206] A close-up reveals a hand holding the yellow pencil over a white paper with printed instructions: "ain ideas supporting your / orting idea). You will need / our essay." The fingers grip the pencil near the tip, poised to write. [206-210] The view transitions to an extreme close-up of a young boy's upper face. He has short dark hair and dark eyebrows, with his eyes cast downward in deep concentration. The camera holds steady on his focused expression without movement.

[210-214] A low-angle shot captures the brick exterior of "MANUEL PEREZ, JR. ELEMENTARY SCHOOL" with a white sign. An American flag flutters on a tall pole against a cloudy blue sky above rows of dark windows. [214-216] The scene shifts to a colorful mural on a light blue cinder block wall, depicting green trees, orange butterflies, flowers, and a figure using a telescope above painted soil and roots. [216-218] A rectangular sign with a red border mounted on a white wall reads "NO SLACKING ANY TIME" in bold red letters. [218-220] In a gymnasium, a red banner hangs on yellow ladder bars featuring a logo, the word "GOAL," and a hockey player. Above it, a handwritten sign lists "STOP," "LOOK," and "LISTEN."

The static shot continues showing an interior brick wall mural of a man with a mustache wearing a dark suit and a green, white, and red sash. To the left, a wooden door features a blue "NOTICE" sign, and a grey building illustration is painted behind the figure. A portion of another colorful mural is visible further down the hallway on the left side.

compressed_segments.text: [0-4] A bald man with black-rimmed glasses, wearing a purple suit jacket, light blue checkered shirt, and blue tie, faces the camera against a blurred hallway background with a green light. He speaks while maintaining eye contact. [4-12] The shot cuts to a close-up profile of the man facing right against a solid red wall. He continues speaking, his expression shifting from a slight smile to an open mouth showing teeth. [12-14] The view returns to the front-facing medium shot in the hallway. The man smiles as white text "15-YEA" appears on the lower right of his jacket.

[14-20] A medium shot captures a bald man with black-rimmed glasses in a purple suit and blue tie against a blurred hallway. Text overlays identify him as "JAMAL MAYER," with the subtitle changing from "15-YEAR NHL VETERAN" to "BLACKHAWKS COMMUNITY LIAISON."
[20-24] The scene cuts to a close-up profile of the man facing right against a red wall, speaking without text.
[24-28] A still photo shows a young boy against a white cinder block wall wearing a red and white jersey with "GEORGE BELL MINOR HOCKEY" text. He holds a hockey glove to his chest and smiles.

[28-30] A static photograph displays a young boy wearing a red and white hockey jersey with "GEORGE BELL MINOR HOCKEY" text, holding a glove against a white cinder block wall. [30-36] The scene cuts to a bald man with thick black-rimmed glasses wearing a purple suit jacket and checkered shirt. He is first shown in close-up profile against a red wall, then in a medium front-facing shot revealing a blue tie and a blurred hallway background. [36-42] The video transitions to a red graphic background featuring the Chicago Blackhawks logo and the text "ORIGINALS." Finally, sponsor branding appears with "AND," the Mazda logo, "MAZDA OF ORLAND PARK," and "PRESENT" overlaid on faint blue hockey rink diagrams.

[42-50] A red title screen displays "MAKING" in black text on the left and "STRIDES" in white text on the right, flanking a central Chicago Blackhawks logo against a background of faint blue hockey rink diagrams. The graphic remains static after an initial transition. [50-54] The visual content cuts abruptly to a solid black screen, removing all text and graphics for the remainder of the clip.

[56-58] The camera zooms out from the brick facade of Manuel Perez, Jr. Elementary School. An American flag waves left while snow covers the ground near a black metal fence right.
[58-62] A static shot shows a light blue mosaic mural of two children studying. Text reads "mis raíces son mi inspiración" at the top and "my roots are my inspiration" along the bottom.
[62-64] The camera focuses on a reddish-brown brick wall with white painted text: "todos vivimos aqui" above "we all live here," with snow at the base.
[64-66] A wooden door features a red apple sign reading "WELCOME" in yellow with Chinese characters. Vertical orange banners with Chinese text hang on the right glass pane.
[66-68] Inside, a hallway mural depicts a man with a mustache in a dark suit wearing a green, white, and red sash. To the left, a door displays a blue "NOTICE" sign.

An American flag flies on a pole to the left, while snow covers the ground and a black metal fence stands on the right. Bright sunlight flares from the upper right corner above the entrance.

The static shot continues showing an interior brick wall mural of a man with a mustache wearing a dark suit and a green, white, and red sash. To the left, a wooden door features a blue "NOTICE" sign, and a grey building illustration is painted behind the figure. A portion of another colorful mural is visible further down the hallway on the left side.

A red graphic background appears featuring faint blue hockey rink diagrams. White text states, "The Get Out and Learn (G.O.A.L.) program has given more than 275,000 Chicago Public School kids access to street hockey equipment and instruction." The number "275,000" is displayed prominently in large, bold white font in the center of the screen.

The static red graphic remains on screen, displaying white text that reads 'The Get Out and Learn (G.O.A.L.) program has given more than 275,000 Chicago Public School kids access to street hockey equipment and instruction.' The large number is centered against a background of faint blue hockey rink diagrams.

On the red background with faint blue diagrams, new white text appears stating "Perez Elementary is one of more than 550 SCHOOLS visited through G.O.A.L." The phrase "550 SCHOOLS" is displayed prominently in large, bold, italicized white font in the center, replacing the previous statistic about program reach.

The red graphic background featuring faint blue hockey diagrams remains unchanged. Centered white text persists, reading "Perez Elementary is one of more than 550 SCHOOLS visited through G.O.A.L." The phrase "550 SCHOOLS" stays prominent in large, bold, italicized font, with no new visual elements or motion occurring during this static interval.

compressed_segments.text:
[0-4] A bald man with black-rimmed glasses, wearing a purple suit jacket, light blue checkered shirt, and blue tie, faces the camera against a blurred hallway background with a green light. He speaks while maintaining eye contact. [4-12] The shot cuts to a close-up profile of the man facing right against a solid red wall. He continues speaking, his expression shifting from a slight smile to an open mouth showing teeth. [12-14] The view returns to the front-facing medium shot in the hallway. The man smiles as white text "15-YEA" appears on the lower right of his jacket. [14-20] A medium shot captures a bald man with black-rimmed glasses in a purple suit and blue tie against a blurred hallway. Text overlays identify him as "JAMAL MAYER," with the subtitle changing from "15-YEAR NHL VETERAN" to "BLACKHAWKS COMMUNITY LIAISON."
[20-24] The scene cuts to a close-up profile of the man facing right against a red wall, speaking without text.
[24-28] A still photo shows a young boy against a white cinder block wall wearing a red and white jersey with "GEORGE BELL MINOR HOCKEY" text. He holds a hockey glove to his chest and smiles.

[28-30] A static photograph displays a young boy wearing a red and white hockey jersey with "GEORGE BELL MINOR HOCKEY" text, holding a glove against a white cinder block wall. [30-36] The scene cuts to a bald man with thick black-rimmed glasses wearing a purple suit jacket and checkered shirt. He is first shown in close-up profile against a red wall, then in a medium front-facing shot revealing a blue tie and a blurred hallway background. [36-42] The video transitions to a red graphic background featuring the Chicago Blackhawks logo and the text "ORIGINALS." Finally, sponsor branding appears with "AND," the Mazda logo, "MAZDA OF ORLAND PARK," and "PRESENT" overlaid on faint blue hockey rink diagrams.

[42-50] A red title screen displays "MAKING" in black text on the left and "STRIDES" in white text on the right, flanking a central Chicago Blackhawks logo against a background of faint blue hockey rink diagrams. The graphic remains static after an initial transition. [50-54] The visual content cuts abruptly to a solid black screen, removing all text and graphics for the remainder of the clip.

[56-58] The camera zooms out from the brick facade of Manuel Perez, Jr. Elementary School. An American flag waves left while snow covers the ground near a black metal fence right.
[58-62] A static shot shows a light blue mosaic mural of two children studying. Text reads "mis raíces son mi inspiración" at the top and "my roots are my inspiration" along the bottom.
[62-64] The camera focuses on a reddish-brown brick wall with white painted text: "todos vivimos aqui" above "we all live here," with snow at the base.
[64-66] A wooden door features a red apple sign reading "WELCOME" in yellow with Chinese characters. Vertical orange banners with Chinese text hang on the right glass pane.
[66-68] Inside, a hallway mural depicts a man with a mustache in a dark suit wearing a green, white, and red sash. To the left, a door displays a blue "NOTICE" sign.

[68-70] A static shot displays an interior brick wall mural of a man with a mustache wearing a dark suit and a green, white, and red sash. To the left, a wooden door features a blue "NOTICE" sign, and a grey building illustration is painted behind the figure. [70-78] A red graphic background appears with faint blue hockey rink diagrams. White text states, "The Get Out and Learn (G.O.A.L.) program has given more than 275,000 Chicago Public School kids access to street hockey equipment and instruction." The number "275,000" is displayed prominently in large, bold white font in the center. The graphic remains static.

He continues speaking, his expression shifting from a slight smile to an open mouth showing teeth. [12-14] The view returns to the front-facing medium shot in the hallway. The man smiles as white text "15-YEA" appears on the lower right of his jacket. [14-20] A medium shot captures a bald man with black-rimmed glasses in a purple suit and blue tie against a blurred hallway. Text overlays identify him as "JAMAL MAYER," with the subtitle changing from "15-YEAR NHL VETERAN" to "BLACKHAWKS COMMUNITY LIAISON." [20-24] The scene cuts to a close-up profile of the man facing right against a red wall, speaking without text. [24-28] A still photo shows a young boy against a white cinder block wall wearing a red and white jersey with "GEORGE BELL MINOR HOCKEY" text. He holds a hockey glove to his chest and smiles. [28-30] A static photograph displays

[42-50] A red title screen displays "MAKING" in black text on the left and "STRIDES" in white text on the right, flanking a central Chicago Blackhawks logo against a background of faint blue hockey rink diagrams. The graphic remains static after an initial transition. [50-54] The visual content cuts abruptly to a solid black screen, removing all text and graphics for the remainder of the clip.

[56-58] The camera zooms out from the brick facade of Manuel Perez, Jr. Elementary School. An American flag waves left while snow covers the ground near a black metal fence right.
[58-62] A static shot shows a light blue mosaic mural of two children studying. Text reads "mis raíces son mi inspiración" at the top and "my roots are my inspiration" along the bottom.
[62-64] The camera focuses on a reddish-brown brick wall with white painted text: "todos vivimos aqui" above "we all live here," with snow at the base.
[64-66] A wooden door features a red apple sign reading "WELCOME" in yellow with Chinese characters. Vertical orange banners with Chinese text hang on the right glass pane.
[66-68] Inside, a hallway mural depicts a man with a mustache in a dark suit wearing a green, white, and red sash. To the left, a door displays a blue "NOTICE" sign.

[68-70] A static shot displays an interior brick wall mural of a man with a mustache wearing a dark suit and a green, white, and red sash. To the left, a wooden door features a blue "NOTICE" sign, and a grey building illustration is painted behind the figure. [70-78] A red graphic background appears with faint blue hockey rink diagrams. White text states, "The Get Out and Learn (G.O.A.L.) program has given more than 275,000 Chicago Public School kids access to street hockey equipment and instruction." The number "275,000" is displayed prominently in large, bold white font in the center. The graphic remains static.

[78-80] A static red graphic displays white text reading 'The Get Out and Learn (G.O.A.L.) program has given more than 275,000 Chicago Public School kids access to street hockey equipment and instruction' against faint blue hockey rink diagrams. [80-84] The text updates to 'Perez Elementary is one of more than 550 SCHOOLS visited through G.O.A.L.,' with '550 SCHOOLS' emphasized in large, bold, italicized font. [84-88] The scene cuts to a classroom where a white t-shirt featuring a 'FIRST STRIDE' logo and Blackhawks emblem hangs in the foreground, while children sit at desks near bright windows in the background. [88-90] The view shifts to students seated at desks from behind, wearing matching navy blue shirts, with one student sporting a long dark braid.

An American flag flies on a pole to the left, while snow covers the ground and a black metal fence stands on the right. Bright sunlight flares from the upper right corner above the entrance.

A young girl with dark hair, wearing a navy blue long-sleeved shirt, sits at a desk holding a yellow pencil in each hand. She looks down at an open notebook while writing. To her left, a silver laptop rests on the wooden desk near a green container holding colorful pens.

A young girl with dark hair in a navy blue shirt sits at a desk holding two yellow pencils. She transitions from looking down at a notebook to lifting her head and looking toward the right with her mouth slightly open. A silver laptop rests on the desk to her left near a green container, while a printer sits in the background.

The camera cuts to an extreme close-up of a hand gripping a yellow pencil, actively writing on a white sheet of paper. The fingers hold the pencil firmly against the page, while other documents lie blurred on the wooden desk surface in the background.

A close-up shot features two children seated side-by-side. The child on the left, wearing a navy blue long-sleeved shirt, rests a hand against their neck with their mouth slightly open. Beside them, the profile of another child with short dark hair is visible, facing forward against a blurred background.

He continues speaking, his expression shifting from a slight smile to an open mouth showing teeth. [12-14] The view returns to the front-facing medium shot in the hallway. The man smiles as white text "15-YEA" appears on the lower right of his jacket. [14-20] A medium shot captures a bald man with black-rimmed glasses in a purple suit and blue tie against a blurred hallway. Text overlays identify him as "JAMAL MAYER," with the subtitle changing from "15-YEAR NHL VETERAN" to "BLACKHAWKS COMMUNITY LIAISON." [20-24] The scene cuts to a close-up profile of the man facing right against a red wall, speaking without text. [24-28] A still photo shows a young boy against a white cinder block wall wearing a red and white jersey with "GEORGE BELL MINOR HOCKEY" text. He holds a hockey glove to his chest and smiles. [28-30] A static photograph displays

[290-294] Near a blue wall with a Chicago Blackhawks goalie graphic, an adult in a grey shirt and cap holds a yellow stick. A boy in a red pinnie bends with a stick beside a boy in a blue shirt and glasses, while a girl in a red pinnie and white shirt bends forward with a hockey stick on the right. [294-296] Against blue gym padding labeled "MANUEL PEREZ JR.", children stand in a line holding hockey sticks. A girl on the right in a navy shirt swings her stick at a white ball, while a boy on the left steps forward. [296-298] A line of young boys wearing red jerseys with a logo stands against a wall featuring blue padding and yellow ladder bars. They hold black hockey sticks vertically with both hands, several with mouths open as if speaking or singing.

[288-290] Two boys in black shirts with "GOAL" text move right across the gym floor gripping hockey sticks, while children in red jerseys watch from the background near red court markings and an adult's legs in grey pants appear on the left. [298-302] A line of young boys wearing red pinnies over navy shirts stands against a white block wall with blue and yellow padding, holding black hockey sticks vertically with white text reading "CHICAGO BLACKHAWKS" on the shafts; their mouths are open as they chant or sing, with the boy on the far right smiling broadly. [302-304] A wide shot displays a gymnasium with blue wall padding where children scatter across the polished floor holding hockey sticks; in the foreground right, a boy in a white polo shirt stands holding a black stick, while other students in navy and white shirts practice drills in the background near red court markings.

[304-306] A wide gymnasium shot displays children practicing floor hockey; in the foreground right, a boy in a white polo shirt holds a black stick near blue and green cones, while background students in navy and white shirts maneuver sticks near red court lines. [306-310] A low-angle shot captures a hockey stick blade near a white ball on the reflective floor, with foreground legs in black pants and red shoes standing beside a yellow cone, as an adult male in a grey t-shirt and red cap observes holding a stick near a doorway. [310-314] The scene shifts to a girl in a white shirt and dark pants swinging a hockey stick to strike a white ball, while an adult male in a grey shirt and beige cap stands opposite holding a yellow stick near blue wall padding, with another person in a grey shirt partially visible on the right edge.

[262-264] Children in navy blue or white shirts and dark pants stand on a gym floor holding hockey sticks near white balls, observed by an adult in a grey shirt. [314-318] A girl in a white shirt and dark pants bends to hit a white ball with a hockey stick; opposite her, a man in a grey shirt and beige cap holds a yellow stick, while a person in a red cap stands near blue wall padding. [318-320] A medium shot shows a man with short brown hair and a beard wearing a navy t-shirt with "I TEACH" text. An overlay reads "MICHAEL MILLIKAN P.E./HEALTH EDUCATION" as he speaks against a blurred blue background.
